channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New laws in NJ impact minimum wage, data privacy, and reading content
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- With every new year come new laws -- each impacting someone somewhere in the Garden State. New Jersey's minimum wage is increasing to $15.49 an hour for most workers.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Former Virginia GOP congressman forms exploratory committee for possible statewide run
item.description: Former Virginia Republican Rep. Denver Riggleman said he has formed an exploratory committee to run for statewide office as an Independent.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things that will shape US politics in 2025
item.description: The fallout of last month’s high-stakes election and recent battles within Congress are offering early clues of what will set the tone for politics in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: The Pentagon chief loses bid to reject 9/11 plea deals
item.description: A military appeals court has ruled against Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin's effort to throw out the plea deals reached for Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two other defendants in the 9/11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DC mayor reports 'great meeting' with Trump after past public feuds, touts 'common ground' on nation's capital
item.description: Washington, D.C., Mayor Muriel Bowser said she met with President-elect Donald Trump to discuss their "shared priorities" for the nation's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The rise in mail voting comes with a price, as mismatched signatures lead to ballot rejections
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — As with many voters on Maui, Joshua Kamalo thought the race for president wasn't the only big contest on the November ballot. He also was focused on a hotly contested seat for the local governing board.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on Sarah McBride, the first openly trans person elected to Congress
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — Sarah McBride made history in Delaware as the first openly transgender state senator in the United States. Now she's making history again, recently elected as the first openly trans member of Congress.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transgender trailblazer Sarah McBride heads to her debut in Congress, hoping for a touch of grace
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — It was her last day in session as a Delaware state senator, and Sarah McBride sat in her tiny office at the state Capitol, preparing farewell remarks. She had made history here, as the first openly transgender state senator in the country.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Dakota senator's son to serve 28 years in prison for crash that killed deputy
item.description: The son of U.S. Sen. Kevin Cramer, R-N.D., will serve 28 years in prison for killing a Mercer County sheriff's deputy during a crash in December 2023 while fleeing law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A guide to funeral services for former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Funeral services honoring former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at age 100, will be held in Georgia and Washington, D.C., beginning Saturday, Jan. 4, and concluding Thursday, Jan. 9. Here's a list of events, as provided by the Joint Task Force-National Capital Region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DNC chair candidate calls for greater investment in GOP states
item.description: In the four-point platform that was first seen by The Hill, Skoufis vowed to “dramatically” enhance the party’s Red State Fund if elected chair.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Allman Brothers’ Chuck Leavell details Jimmy Carter’s musical legacy
item.description: "He was such a likable human being," said Chuck Leavell, former Allman Brothers Band member and current Rolling Stones touring keyboardist.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A 34-year-old soldier serving with the Indiana National Guard in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident, the Army said Monday. Capt. Eric Richard Hart, 34, of Indianapolis, Indiana, died Saturday in Iraq, the Army National Guard said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden closes government as 'mark of respect' for late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Monday signed an executive order closing executive offices of the government for late former President Jimmy Carter's funeral on Jan. 9.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump's Vote of Confidence
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker math: Breaking down how many votes Johnson needs to keep his gavel
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) heads to the House floor on Friday in hopes of being formally reelected Speaker. While Johnson has the endorsement of President-elect Trump to remain Speaker, one Republican is pledging to oppose him, and several others are not committing to supporting Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An appeals court upholds a $5 million award in a sexual abuse verdict against President-elect Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal appeals court on Monday upheld a jury’s finding in a civil case that Donald Trump sexually abused a columnist in an upscale department store dressing room in the mid-1990s. The 2nd U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump endorses Mike Johnson to stay on as House Speaker despite government funding turmoil
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday endorsed House Speaker Mike Johnson, providing crucial backing for the Louisiana Republican as he prepares for what is expected to be another contentious speakership race this week.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A caucus of military veterans seeks to bridge the political divide in a polarized Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The players in this year's Army-Navy football game kept up a long tradition, with each side honoring the other’s school song when the contest ended. It was an acknowledgment that the future could bring moments when the opponents that day are teammates on deadlier fields.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How American presidents have planned their own funerals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter's memorial journey will end at his house in the tiny town of Plains, Georgia, where he grew up on a peanut farm. That is where his wife, Rosalynn, was laid to rest last year in a burial plot that they chose years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter 'was most impactful one-term president': Stuart Eizenstat
item.description: He also credited Carter for initiating defense programs that would later be associated with the Reagan administration.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Geraldo Rivera recalls Jimmy Carter's life, legacy
item.description: "He was a wonderful president and even better in his post-presidential life," Geraldo Rivera said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another Florida state representative switches from Democrat to Republican
item.description: For the second time this year, a Florida Democratic lawmaker has switched their party affiliation after winning their seat.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
